# Source Index

| ID | Field | Title | Key finding | Supports | URL |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| S01 | Accessibility / Captions | W3C WAI: Captions/Subtitles | Captions are synchronized text versions of speech and needed non-speech audio. | DLC, CCP, NSS, accessibility guardrails | https://www.w3.org/WAI/media/av/captions/ |
| S02 | Caption speed / Standards | Section 508: Captions and Transcripts | Speech above about 180 words per minute may be too fast for captions; keep captions short. | SCC, CRL, MEG, VLAC | https://www.section508.gov/create/captions-transcripts/ |
| S03 | Sound-off viewing | Szarkowska et al. 2024, PLOS ONE: sound-off subtitled video | Sound-off subtitled viewing increased cognitive load and reduced comprehension, immersion and enjoyment; viewers processed subtitles more heavily. | MFM, DLC, SCC, BCC+ | https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0306251 |
| S04 | Subtitle speed / Eye tracking | Kruger et al.: Why subtitle speed matters | Higher subtitle speed can reduce comprehension, increase incomplete reading and reduce rereading. | SCC, CRL, MEG, MRR, VLAC | https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/applied-psycholinguistics/article/why-subtitle-speed-matters-evidence-from-word-skipping-and-rereading/A7DB0CBF5910353143738CF9C1721317 |
| S05 | Partial captions | Mirzaei et al. 2017: Partial and Synchronized Captioning | Partial synchronized captions produced full-caption-level comprehension while presenting less than 30% of transcript in an L2 context. | SCC, CRL, KMC, GET | https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/recall/article/partial-and-synchronized-captioning-a-new-tool-to-assist-learners-in-developing-second-language-listening-skill/9394E3F0AD25FD5F32895F965F7ECCAD |
| S06 | Keyword highlighting / DHH captions | Kafle et al. 2019 ASSETS: Highlighting key words in captions | Highlighted keywords were rated easier to read/follow and lower task load by DHH participants. | SKH, FOA, BCA, KHT | https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3308561.3353781 |
| S07 | Multimedia learning / Signaling | Schneider et al. 2018: Signaling meta-analysis | Signaling benefits retention and transfer, affects eye fixations and reduces cognitive load. | OFC, PCFB, TBSM, SCC, BCC+ | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1747938X17300581 |
| S08 | Multimedia learning / Signaling | Richter et al. 2016: text-picture signaling meta-analysis | Cues that highlight organization of relevant information can improve retention/transfer. | PCFB, TBSM, REN, CEP | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1747938X15000664 |
| S09 | Meaning-guided attention | Henderson & Hayes 2018: Meaning maps and attention | Meaning maps predict attention in real-world scenes beyond low-level salience. | MMP, SAP, GSP, ASOE | https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-018-31894-5 |
| S10 | Visual crowding | Schwetlick & Harrison 2024/2025 review: Visual Crowding | Crowding impairs perception when objects/letters are surrounded by clutter. | CAC, CBU, STB, CCD | https://www.annualreviews.org/content/journals/10.1146/annurev-vision-110423-024409 |
| S11 | Peripheral/foveal vision | Stewart et al. 2020: peripheral and foveal interactions | Peripheral and foveal signals are integrated across saccades; detailed recognition depends on foveal/parafoveal sampling. | FAP, GSP, VLAW | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7645222/ |
| S12 | Visual working memory | Luck & Vogel 2013: Visual working memory capacity | Visual working memory is capacity-limited and varies across individuals. | OIOS, MRR, PCFB, TBSM | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1364661313001265 |
| S13 | Audiovisual speech | Dias et al. 2016: Visibility of speech articulation | Visual speech information can compensate when auditory speech is degraded. | MSC, ESC, GSP | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8081648/ |
| S14 | Bionic Reading | Snell 2024: No, Bionic Reading does not work | No significant reading-time benefit between Bionic and normal text. | BCA as weak/optional; HBF negative control | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0001691824001811 |
| S15 | Bionic Reading / Eye tracking | Možina et al. 2025: Usability of Bionic Reading | No clear speed/comprehension advantage; bionic formatting produced more saccades and longer reading time in some conditions. | HBF negative control; BCA dose testing | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/21582440251376158 |
| S16 | Dynamic subtitles | Brown et al. 2015: Dynamic Subtitles user experience | Dynamic subtitles produced gaze patterns closer to no-subtitle baseline than traditional subtitles in the study. | SFC, GSP, DRAR | https://research.manchester.ac.uk/en/publications/dynamic-subtitles-the-user-experience/ |
| S17 | Video saliency prediction | AIM 2024 / AViMoS video saliency prediction challenge | AViMoS provides 1,500 videos with observer data, including vertical videos; saliency prediction is technically mature enough to inform placement. | SHP, SAP, ASOE, VLP | https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.14827 |
| S18 | Kinetic typography | Lee 2023: Learning benefits of moving text | Moving text can help when aligned with logic, but excessive/inappropriate kinetic typography can cause fatigue and overload. | LM, PRL, DKC, MBS | https://www.nature.com/articles/s41599-023-01646-6 |
| S19 | Affective captions | Caption Royale 2024: affective captions for DHH users | Affective captions use typographic modulation to convey emotion; explored with DHH participants. | PAE, TI, affective-semantic variants | https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3613904.3642258 |
| S20 | Subtitle segmentation | Gerber-Morón et al. 2018: subtitle line breaks | Viewers preferred syntactically segmented line breaks; linguistic segmentation affects processing. | SLB, OIOS, SLSB | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7733619/ |
| S21 | TikTok subtitle style | Duraj & Szarkowska 2025: non-standard TikTok subtitle styles | TikTok subtitle style with emojis/non-standard typography affected preference and engagement in reported studies. | PNT, ENS, platform-native variants | https://www.jatjournal.org/index.php/jat/article/view/339 |
| S22 | Short-form cognition | Alruwaili 2025: scroll immersion and short-form video use | Short-form use and scroll immersion predicted attention difficulty, working-memory disruption and cognitive fatigue in survey data. | FPR, HOO, MRR, ERF | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0001691825009874 |
| S23 | Phrase structure / Rapid language | Fallon et al. 2024 Science Advances: Language at a glance | The left temporal cortex detects rough syntactic structure starting as early as 125 ms after visual multiword expression onset. | OFC, CPC, PCFB, KMC | https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adr9951 |
| S24 | A/B testing | Nielsen Norman Group 2024: A/B Testing 101 | A/B testing compares design variations with live audiences against predetermined success metrics. | ABG, proof ladder, platform validation | https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ab-testing/ |

| S27 | Preregistration | Lakens 2024: benefits of preregistration and Registered Reports | Registered Reports move peer review earlier so feedback happens before data collection. | proof contract, prereg templates | https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/2833373X.2024.2376046 |
| S28 | Equivalence / non-inferiority | Kishore et al. 2020: superiority, noninferiority, equivalence | Non-inferiority/equivalence designs answer different questions from superiority designs and require margins. | accessibility non-regression and visual recall guardrails | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7734976/ |
| S29 | Contrast accessibility | WCAG 2.2 contrast minimum understanding | Sufficient contrast is needed so text can be read by people with moderately low vision or contrast impairment. | ACAC, FLCC, CBU | https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/contrast-minimum.html |
